name: Ping System
description: Ping System is a social or networked mechanic that coordinates multiple players within shared rules.
design_notes: Ping System should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For multiplayer mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use ping system as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect ping system to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: Players feel coordinated, competitive, or socially expressive within shared rules.

parameters.name: player_count
parameters.description: Number of players supported by the mechanic.

parameters.name: sync_rate
parameters.description: Frequency at which state updates are exchanged or resolved.

parameters.name: failure_timeout
parameters.description: Time before the mechanic handles disconnects or missing responses.

edge_cases: Ping System is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Ping System overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Ping System runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Ping System state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Ping System plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Ping System produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Ping System strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Ping System becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Ping System with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Ping System as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Ping System with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Ping System as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.

example_games.title: Apex Legends
example_games.note: Uses contextual pings for non-verbal team communication.
